# Adaptive Scheduler

A voice-first reminder app that goes beyond "set a timer" — it classifies reminders, decides if they need pre-alerts or follow-ups, and can actually reason about your schedule in conversation.

## The problem
Most reminder apps just fire a notification and forget about you. This one tries to think a step further: an exam needs prep time before it fires, a task might deserve a check-in after, and "remind me after my exam" only makes sense if the agent already knows when the exam is.

## Architecture
Started as one large `agent.py` file. Split it into focused pieces once complexity grew:

- `router.py` — plain keyword matching decides create/update/delete/query, no LLM call needed for this part
- `context.py` — pulls the user's timezone before the agent starts reasoning
- `tools.py` — the only code allowed to touch the database
- `prompts.py` — one prompt per workflow instead of one giant prompt handling everything
- `orchestrator.py` — ties it together

The LLM never touches the database directly. It only says "call this tool with these arguments" — a separate layer of plain Python does the real work, which keeps the risky part (DB writes) testable independent of anything AI-related.

## Interesting problems

**Duration doesn't mean the same thing for every task.** An exam has a fixed length. Reading a book doesn't. Picking a hackathon problem statement has no sensible duration at all. Reminders get sorted into instant / externally-timed / self-paced / open-ended buckets, each handled differently — and the agent asks about duration at most once, then moves on with a default instead of pestering.

**Staying stateless on purpose.** The backend doesn't hold conversation state between messages — the full history gets resent every time and context gets rebuilt from scratch. Feels wasteful, but it means the server scales without juggling sessions, and it's also what lets the agent notice "I already asked this" without a separate memory system.

**Picking a free cron host.** Needed something to ping a `/cron/check-reminders` endpoint every minute. UptimeRobot's free tier only checks every 5 minutes — too slow for on-time firing. Render's native cron has a minimum charge. Landed on cron-job.org, which runs every-minute jobs for free.

## What I'm most proud of
Asking the agent "do I have a gap today" and getting back an answer computed from real reminders between 7AM–6PM, not a guess. Same for "did I attend the wedding last month" — it searches past reminders and matches even when the phrasing doesn't match the original title.

## Stack
Python, FastAPI, Supabase, vanilla JS frontend, hosted on Render.

## What's next
Reminder tones, vibration mode fixes, and more of the conversational schedule-query features.
